simple_functions.label: Simple Functions
simple_functions.summary: Learn how simple SQL functions transform text, numbers, and dates so results become cleaner and more useful.

sketch3.title: What should beginners know about date functions?

intro_to_functions.title: A function is a built-in tool that transforms a value.
intro_to_functions.bodyMarkdown:
A SQL **function** takes a value, does something to it, and returns a result.

Functions are useful when you want to:

- change text
- round numbers
- work with dates

So the big idea is:

> a function helps SQL transform values in the result

Like expressions, functions do not need to permanently change the table.
They help shape the query output.

string_functions.title: String functions help change or inspect text values.
string_functions.bodyMarkdown:
String functions work with text.

Beginner examples include:

- making text uppercase
- making text lowercase
- checking text length

These are useful when you want more consistent or informative output.

So the key idea is:

> string functions help format or inspect text values

number_functions.title: Number functions help adjust or clean numeric results.
number_functions.bodyMarkdown:
Number functions are useful when you want numeric output to be easier to read.

A beginner example is rounding.

That is helpful when a calculated value has more detail than you want to show.

So the big idea is:

> number functions help shape numeric output into a cleaner form

date_function_awareness.title: Date functions help work with date values in a more deliberate way.
date_function_awareness.bodyMarkdown:
Dates often need special handling.

Even when a date already looks readable, SQL functions can still help you treat it as a date value in the result.

A beginner does not need to master every date function yet.

The important idea is:

- dates are their own kind of value
- SQL has built-in tools for them too

So a good beginner mindset is:

> be aware that dates can be transformed or checked with functions, just like text and numbers

m5_s03_uppercase_customer_names.title: Use a string function
m5_s03_uppercase_customer_names.prompt:
The SQLite workspace already has a table named `orders`.

Write a query that returns `customer_name` and another result column that shows `customer_name` in uppercase.

Run it and compare the two columns.

help.concept: A string function can transform text into a different form in the result.
help.hint_1: Select the original `customer_name`, then use a text function on that same column to create an uppercase version.
help.hint_2: Build the query with the original column first, then add one more selected value that applies an uppercase text function to it.

m5_s03_uppercase_customer_names.hint: Use an uppercase string function on `customer_name`.
m5_s03_uppercase_customer_names.starterCode: -- Show customer_name and an uppercase version of it

m5_s03_lowercase_category.title: Use another string function
m5_s03_lowercase_category.prompt:
The `orders` table already exists.

Write a query that returns `category` and another result column that shows `category` in lowercase.

Then click **Run**.

help.concept: String functions can change the letter case of text values.
help.hint_1: Select `category`, then apply a lowercase text function to that same column.
help.hint_2: Keep the original text column in the result, and add one transformed version beside it using a lowercase string function.

m5_s03_lowercase_category.hint: Use a lowercase string function on `category`.
m5_s03_lowercase_category.starterCode: -- Show category and a lowercase version of it

m5_s03_name_length.title: Measure text length
m5_s03_name_length.prompt:
The `orders` table already exists.

Write a query that returns `customer_name` and another result column showing the length of each `customer_name`.

Run it and inspect the numeric results.

help.concept: Some string functions inspect text instead of changing its letters.
help.hint_1: Select `customer_name`, then use a function that counts how many characters are in it.
help.hint_2: Build the query with the original name column and one extra selected value that measures that text.

m5_s03_name_length.starterCode: -- Show customer_name and the length of customer_name

m5_s03_round_total_price.title: Use a number function
m5_s03_round_total_price.prompt:
The `orders` table already exists.

Write a query that returns `customer_name` and a calculated total using `quantity` and `unit_price`, but round that total to 2 decimal places in the result.

Then click **Run**.

help.concept: Number functions can make calculated results cleaner, such as by rounding them.
help.hint_1: First think about the total expression, then wrap that calculated value in a rounding function.
help.hint_2: Build the total from the two numeric columns, then apply a number function that keeps only 2 decimal places.

m5_s03_round_total_price.starterCode: -- Show customer_name and a rounded total price

m5_s03_date_awareness_query.title: Use a date-aware function
m5_s03_date_awareness_query.prompt:
The `orders` table already exists.

Write a query that returns `customer_name`, `order_date`, and another result column that applies a date function to `order_date`.

Run it and notice that SQL also has functions for dates.

help.concept: Date functions help SQL treat date-like values more deliberately.
help.hint_1: Select the original `order_date`, then add one more selected value that applies a date-oriented function to that same column.
help.hint_2: Build the query with the original date column visible, then add one transformed version beside it using a beginner-friendly date function.

m5_s03_date_awareness_query.starterCode: -- Show customer_name, order_date, and a date-function version of order_date

m5_s03_functions_project.title: Practice simple functions
m5_s03_functions_project.prompt:
Use the SQL workspace on the right to practice simple functions on the `orders` table.

For each task:

1. type the query in the editor
2. click **Run**
3. read the **Results** tab
4. compare the original value with the transformed value

Tasks:

1. Show `customer_name` and an uppercase version of it
2. Show `category` and a lowercase version of it
3. Show `customer_name` and the length of each name
4. Show a rounded total using `quantity` and `unit_price`
5. Show `order_date` and a date-function version of it

Run one query at a time.
This project is about using simple functions to shape query output.

help.concept: Functions transform values in the result so output becomes easier to work with or read.
help.hint_1: Each task keeps the same idea: show the original value, then show a transformed version created by a function.
help.hint_2: Solve the tasks one by one. Decide whether the value is text, number, or date-like, then choose a matching function and place it in the select list.

m5_s03_functions_project.hint: Show the original value and a transformed version.
m5_s03_functions_project.starterCode:
-- Task 1
-- Show customer_name and an uppercase version of it

m5_s03_function_meaning.title: What is a SQL function?
m5_s03_function_meaning.prompt: What is the main job of a function in a SQL query?

help.concept: A function transforms or inspects a value and returns a result.
help.hint_2: It helps change, shape, or inspect data in the query output.

options.a: It transforms or inspects a value in the query
options.d: It removes the need for columns

m5_s03_string_functions_meaning.title: What do string functions help with?
m5_s03_string_functions_meaning.prompt: What are string functions mainly used for in beginner SQL?

help.concept: String functions work with text values.
help.hint_2: These functions help format or inspect text.

m5_s03_number_functions_meaning.title: What do number functions help with?
m5_s03_number_functions_meaning.prompt: What are number functions mainly used for in beginner SQL?

help.concept: Number functions help clean or shape numeric output.
help.hint_2: They help make numeric results easier to read or use.

m5_s03_date_functions_awareness.title: What should beginners know about date functions?
m5_s03_date_functions_awareness.prompt: What is a good beginner idea to remember about date functions in SQL?

help.concept: Dates are a special kind of value, and SQL has built-in tools for working with them.
help.hint_1: Think dates are not just random text forever.
help.hint_2: The key awareness is that SQL can also transform or inspect date-like values.

m5_s03_date_functions_awareness.hint: Think SQL has tools for dates too.

options.a: SQL has built-in functions for working with date values too
options.b: Dates can never appear in queries
options.c: Date functions only work in spreadsheets
options.d: Dates always behave exactly like aliases

m5_s03_reading_function_output.title: How should you read function output?
m5_s03_reading_function_output.prompt: What is a good beginner way to read a query result that includes a function?

help.concept: A strong habit is to compare the original value with the transformed value returned by the function.
help.hint_2: Check whether the transformed result matches what the function is supposed to do.

options.a: Compare the original value with the transformed value
options.d: Assume the function worked without checking

m5_s03_functions_true.title: What is true about SQL functions?
m5_s03_functions_true.prompt: Select all true statements about functions in beginner SQL.

help.concept: Functions help transform or inspect values inside the query result.
help.hint_2: Choose the statements about shaping values in the output.

options.c: Functions can be used in selected results
options.e: Functions can help make output more useful

m5_s03_string_true.title: What is true about string functions?
m5_s03_string_true.prompt: Select all true statements about string functions in beginner SQL.

help.concept: String functions work with text values.
help.hint_2: Choose the statements about formatting or examining text.

options.a: String functions can change letter case
options.b: String functions can inspect text length
options.c: String functions work with text values
options.d: String functions are only for deleting rows
options.e: String functions can make text output easier to read

m5_s03_number_true.title: What is true about number functions?
m5_s03_number_true.prompt: Select all true statements about number functions in beginner SQL.

help.concept: Number functions help shape numeric results.
help.hint_1: Think rounding or cleaning a calculation.
help.hint_2: Choose the statements about making numeric output easier to work with.

options.a: Number functions can help round calculated values
options.b: Number functions can make numeric output cleaner
options.c: Number functions can be used in selected results
options.e: Number functions are useful in report-style queries

m5_s03_date_true.title: What is true about date functions?
m5_s03_date_true.prompt: Select all true statements about date functions in beginner SQL.

help.concept: Date functions help SQL work with date-like values more deliberately.
help.hint_1: Think dates are a value type SQL can work with.
help.hint_2: Choose the statements about date transformation or date awareness.

options.a: SQL can use functions on date-like values
options.b: Date functions can appear in selected results
options.c: Dates are worth treating carefully in queries
options.d: Date functions make text functions impossible
options.e: Beginners should know that SQL has date tools too

m5_s03_function_workflow_true.title: What matches a good beginner workflow for using functions?
m5_s03_function_workflow_true.prompt: Select all statements that match a good beginner workflow for function queries.

help.concept: A good workflow is to identify the value type, choose the right function, run the query, and verify the output.
help.hint_2: Choose the steps that help you transform values on purpose.

m5_s03_function_workflow_true.hint: Choose the right function for the value type.

options.a: Identify whether the value is text, number, or date-like
options.b: Choose a function that matches that value type
options.c: Run the query and inspect the transformed output
options.d: Compare the transformed result with the original value
options.e: Ignore whether the function matches the value type

m5_s03_fill_function_returns.title: What a function returns
m5_s03_fill_function_returns.prompt: Fill in the blank with the best word.
m5_s03_fill_function_returns.template: A SQL function takes a value, transforms it, and returns a ____.
m5_s03_fill_function_returns.hint: A function returns a transformed value.

help.concept: Functions transform a value and give back a result.
help.hint_2: The missing word is `result`.

m5_s03_fill_uppercase_fn.title: Uppercase function
m5_s03_fill_uppercase_fn.prompt: Fill in the blank with the function name.
m5_s03_fill_uppercase_fn.template: To make text uppercase in SQL, use ____.
m5_s03_fill_uppercase_fn.hint: Use the string function that capitalizes text.

help.concept: UPPER transforms text to uppercase.
help.hint_2: The correct function is `UPPER`.

m5_s03_fill_lowercase_fn.title: Lowercase function
m5_s03_fill_lowercase_fn.prompt: Fill in the blank with the function name.
m5_s03_fill_lowercase_fn.template: To make text lowercase in SQL, use ____.
m5_s03_fill_lowercase_fn.hint: Use the string function that makes text lowercase.

help.concept: LOWER transforms text to lowercase.
help.hint_2: The correct function is `LOWER`.

m5_s03_fill_rounding_fn.title: Rounding function
m5_s03_fill_rounding_fn.prompt: Fill in the blank with the function name.
m5_s03_fill_rounding_fn.template: To round a numeric result in SQL, use ____.
m5_s03_fill_rounding_fn.hint: Use the function that cleans up decimal output.

help.concept: ROUND helps shape numeric output into a cleaner form.
help.hint_2: The correct function is `ROUND`.

m5_s03_fill_compare_output.title: How to read function output
m5_s03_fill_compare_output.prompt: Fill in the blank with the best word.
m5_s03_fill_compare_output.template: A good beginner habit is to compare the transformed value with the ____ value.
m5_s03_fill_compare_output.hint: You should compare before and after.

help.concept: Checking the original value helps you understand what the function changed.
help.hint_2: The missing word is `original`.

m5_s03_reorder_function_workflow.title: Order the function workflow
m5_s03_reorder_function_workflow.prompt: Reorder the steps into a good beginner workflow for using functions in a query.

help.concept: A good workflow helps you choose and verify the right transformation.
help.hint_1: Decide the value type before choosing the function.
help.hint_2: Comparison comes after running the query.

m5_s03_reorder_upper_pattern.title: Order an uppercase pattern
m5_s03_reorder_upper_pattern.prompt: Reorder the parts into a common uppercase function pattern.
m5_s03_reorder_upper_pattern.hint: Function name first, then parentheses around the column.

help.concept: String functions wrap a text column in function-call syntax.
help.hint_2: Then open the parentheses.

m5_s03_reorder_round_pattern.title: Order a rounding pattern
m5_s03_reorder_round_pattern.prompt: Reorder the parts into a common rounding pattern.
m5_s03_reorder_round_pattern.hint: Function name first, then expression and decimal places.

help.concept: ROUND can wrap a calculated expression and keep a chosen number of decimal places.
help.hint_2: The decimal-place argument comes last.

m5_s03_reorder_read_output.title: Order how to read function output
m5_s03_reorder_read_output.prompt: Reorder the steps into a good way to read function results.

tokens.t3: check whether the function did the expected change

m5_s03_reorder_read_output.hint: Read the original value before judging the transformed one.

help.concept: Comparing original and transformed values helps confirm the function worked as expected.
help.hint_2: Then judge the change.

m5_s03_reorder_value_types.title: Order the value types
m5_s03_reorder_value_types.prompt: Reorder the value categories in the same sequence emphasized in this lesson.

help.concept: This lesson introduces text, number, and date-like values as the main beginner categories.
help.hint_2: Date awareness comes last.
